summary: Maria Santos and Ananya Sharma from Aegis met with Gerald Hutchins of Forge Industries to introduce Comply v2, focusing on its PCI DSS reporting capabilities and multi-framework compliance support. Gerald shared that Forge is in PCI DSS audit prep with a QSA assessment scheduled for end of June, struggling with a highly manual evidence-gathering process. The team covered on-demand reporting, auditor-friendly export formats, multi-framework overlap between PCI DSS and SOC 2, and an estimated two-to-three week onboarding timeline. Gerald also raised prior concerns about a March Detect outage, which Maria acknowledged and committed to addressing with a full post-incident review.
actionItems: Maria Santos: Send Gerald the Detect post-incident review and Comply v2 product brief by end of day
Ananya Sharma: Prepare a customized PCI DSS to SOC 2 control mapping one-pager for Gerald's internal conversations
Maria Santos: Schedule a technical deep-dive demo session with Gerald's team and Ananya for approximately one week out
Gerald Hutchins: Share the post-incident review materials with his steering committee to address internal concerns about Detect reliability

keyMoments.text: Gerald expresses frustration with his current manual PCI DSS evidence-gathering process, noting it consumes weeks of engineering time and prolongs QSA interactions.
keyMoments.speaker: Gerald Hutchins

keyMoments.text: Ananya explains the multi-framework control overlap in Comply v2, revealing that PCI DSS evidence artifacts can automatically satisfy SOC 2 criteria without re-instrumentation.
keyMoments.speaker: Ananya Sharma

keyMoments.text: Gerald reveals that earlier reliability concerns about Detect came up in his steering committee, signaling potential churn risk tied to the March outage.
keyMoments.speaker: Gerald Hutchins

keyMoments.text: Maria proactively acknowledges the March Detect outage, describes architectural remediation steps taken, and offers to share the full post-incident review.
keyMoments.speaker: Maria Santos
